pi_items.source: Verified in scenario file: '~/Diagrams/gitflow_release.drawio' expands to /home/ga when user='ga'. Confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 89-90) and export_result.sh (line 8). Directory created by setup script at line 7.
Verified in scenario file: '~/Diagrams/gitflow_release.png' expands to /home/ga when user='ga'. Confirmed in export_result.sh (line 9).

Verified in scenario file (setup_task.sh lines 73-80). Scenario explicitly lists 'SUMMARY OF BRANCHES (7 total)' with all 7 branch names matching exactly. Each branch is referenced in the timeline (T1-T10).

Verified in scenario file: (1) v2.3.1 tagged at T1 - 'main branch has tag v2.3.1'; (2) v2.3.2 tagged at T9 - 'hotfix/2.3.2 merged into main → tag v2.3.2'; (3) v2.4.0 tagged at T10 - 'release/2.4.0 merged into main → tag v2.4.0'.

Verified in scenario file DIAGRAM CONVENTIONS section (lines 24-28): #2196F3 (BLUE for main), #4CAF50 (GREEN for develop), #FF9800 (ORANGE for feature/*), #9C27B0 (PURPLE for release/*), #F44336 (RED for hotfix/*). All are valid Material Design color codes.

Reasonable minimum based on diagram content analysis: 10 commits (C1-C10 as per timeline T2-T10), 7 branch lines (main, develop, feature/user-preferences, feature/payment-retry, feature/audit-logging, release/2.4.0, hotfix/2.3.2), 3 tags (v2.3.1, v2.3.2, v2.4.0), plus multiple merge arrows and branch creation arrows. Total estimated elements: 30+, so minimum of 25 is conservative and verifiable.

Verified based on merge and branch operations in scenario: At least 6 feature branch merges to develop, 1 hotfix merge to main, 1 hotfix merge to develop, 1 release merge to main, 1 release merge to develop, plus 7 branch creation arrows from parent branches. Total: 17+ connections, exceeding minimum of 12.

Scenario timeline (T2-T10) lists commits C1 through C10: 2 in feature/user-preferences, 3 in feature/payment-retry, 1 in feature/audit-logging, 2 in release/2.4.0 initial, 1 in hotfix/2.3.2, 1 in release/2.4.0 continued.

Scenario implements correct GitFlow model: main branch has release tags, develop is integration branch, features branch from develop and merge back, release branches merge to both main and develop, hotfix branches created from main and merge to both main and develop.

privileged_info_summary: The GitFlow Release Branching task involves creating a diagram showing the CloudStore API Platform's release cycle for version 2.4.0. The diagram must contain exactly 7 branches (main, develop, 3 feature branches, 1 release branch, 1 hotfix branch), 3 version tags (v2.3.1, v2.3.2, v2.4.0), and 10 commits arranged chronologically left-to-right. The diagram uses Material Design colors: #2196F3 (blue) for main, #4CAF50 (green) for develop, #FF9800 (orange) for features, #9C27B0 (purple) for release, #F44336 (red) for hotfix. Files must be saved to /home/ga/Diagrams/ as gitflow_release.drawio (source) and gitflow_release.png (export). The scenario is synthetic and created by the task setup script.
